preview_text:
The model favors Germany at 35.3%, but only marginally. Ecuador's win probability of 33.1% keeps the match genuinely competitive, with the two teams separated by just 2 percentage points in the model's assessment. Germany enters as the statistical favorite, yet the gap is narrow enough that either side has a credible path to victory.

The draw rate of 31.5% suggests this match will likely be closely contested. The spread across outcomes—with no single result commanding a decisive majority—indicates relatively balanced underlying strength. A draw remains plausible, and the three outcomes cluster within a tight range, pointing to a match where tactical execution and marginal factors could determine the result.

The market view diverges substantially from the model. Sportsbooks price Germany at 56.1% and Ecuador at just 19.2%, a 16.9 percentage point gap favoring the Germans. Polymarket prices align closely with the consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better predicts the outcome; the divergence flags that market participants assign materially higher confidence to Germany than the model's forecast does.
